opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I literally cannot hold my head up, my voice is gone, and my brain feels like it's shut down completely.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly the moment I got home from a long work networking night — I'm scared something is really wrong with me.

symptom_narrative.onset: suddenly, right after I walked in the door tonight following hours at a work event
symptom_narrative.duration: the last hour or two, since I got home
symptom_narrative.character: my whole body feels like lead and I can barely move, my voice has gone to a strained whisper and my throat is tight, and my thinking feels slow and foggy, like my brain switched off
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've felt — I collapsed onto the couch still in my work clothes and can't get up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: everywhere — my head, my body, my voice
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: hits me hard the instant I get home after events like this, then eases over a day or two of hiding away
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: being completely alone and silent afterwards for a long time
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: hours of having to carefully change how I talk, my accent and my pitch, and watch how I act so I fit in and feel safe at these events
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my neck and throat feel tense and sore from holding my voice a certain way all night; I feel wrung out and resentful that I have to perform like this
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: this heavy crash and vocal exhaustion happens after big social or professional events, but tonight felt more sudden and severe
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: right now I can't safely move around my own home; I need to shut off from everyone for a while to recover

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I spend a lot of energy at work and social events changing how I speak and behave so I'm accepted and feel safe, and I feel like I've lost track of who I really am; afterwards I need to completely isolate, and I dread my different worlds colliding. I live on my own.

communication_style.typical_message_style: articulate but exhausted; anxious about how clearly she is expressing herself

objective_data_offered.value: my voice is barely a whisper and my throat feels tight and strained
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-description over chat; not a clinician assessment of the voice or airway

objective_data_offered.value: I'm slumped on the couch and feel too heavy to lift my head or stand up safely
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported functional state; strength and neurological function not objectively tested
